# Pull Request

## Description

Brief description of what this PR does.

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] New skill
- [ ] Improvement to existing skill
- [ ] New framework
- [ ] Framework correction/improvement

List specific changes:

-

## Testing

How was this tested?
- [ ] Tested with Claude Code
- [ ] Tested with Claude Desktop
- [ ] Verified output format
- [ ] Tested reference file links
- [ ] Checked documentation
- [ ] Framework tested via `/use-framework` (if applicable)
- [ ] Verified against quality criteria (for new frameworks)

## For New Frameworks

If adding a new framework:
- [ ] Framework is real and documented (not AI-generated)
- [ ] Includes creator/origin and date
- [ ] Has clear methodology and steps
- [ ] Contains real-world examples
- [ ] Includes sources and references
- [ ] Updated `frameworks-index.md`
- [ ] Tested with `/use-framework`
- [ ] Added to both `frameworks/` and `skills/use-framework/references/frameworks/`

## Example

Show an example of the improvement in action (if applicable):

```
[Example dialogue, framework application, or output]
```

## Related Issues

Fixes #(issue number)
Related to #(issue number)

## Checklist

- [ ] Code follows existing style and structure
- [ ] Documentation updated (if needed)
- [ ] Changes tested with Claude
- [ ] No breaking changes (or documented if yes)
- [ ] Commits have clear messages
- [ ] References properly linked

## Additional Notes

Any other context or information reviewers should know.
